articles.title: BEAMS: Benchmarking and Evaluating AI for Modeling and Simulation
articles.description:
arXiv:2605.28994v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI tools to support real world decision making must be able to build simulation models that inform their recommendations and render them interpretable. Tools that can automate aspects of modeling practice must complement human expertise, not replace it. The BEAMS Initiative aims to guide the development of AI tools for modeling and simulation toward forms that are responsible and ethical by establishing benchmarks for human centered modeling and s
articles.title_zh: BEAMS: Benchmarking and Evaluating AI for Modeling and Simulation

articles.title: Advance Trustworthy AI and ML, and Identify Best Practices for Scaling AI
articles.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor   Advancing trustworthy AI and machine learning to mitigate agency risk is a priority for the US Department of Energy (DOE), and identifying best practices for implementing AI at scale is a priority for the US General Services Administration (GSA).   That’s what attendees learned in two sessions at the AI […]]]>
articles.title_zh: Advance Trustworthy AI and ML, and Identify Best Practices for Scaling AI

articles.title: Review Arcade: On the Human Alignment and Gameability of LLM Reviews
articles.description:
arXiv:2605.28897v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLM-generated reviews for scientific papers are gaining considerable traction and are even being officially piloted by major conferences. We have to assume that not only reviewers are using LLM-assistance, but also that authors use LLMs to revise their papers before submitting. In this work, we perform empirical experiments on papers from the 2025 ACL Rolling Review (ARR) to evaluate LLM reviews from both the author and the reviewer perspective. F
articles.title_zh: Review Arcade: On the Human Alignment and Gameability of LLM Reviews

articles.description: By John P. articles.title: How Accountability Practices Are Pursued by AI Engineers in the Federal Government
articles.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor    Two experiences of how AI developers within the federal government are pursuing AI accountability practices were outlined at the AI World Government event held virtually and in-person this week in Alexandria, Va.  Taka Ariga, chief data scientist and director at the US Government Accountability Office, described an AI accountability framework he uses within his agency […]]]>

articles.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor   Engineers tend to see things in unambiguous terms, which some may call Black and White terms, such as a choice between right or wrong and good and bad. The consideration of ethics in AI is highly nuanced, with vast gray areas, making it  challenging for AI software engineers to […]]]>
articles.title_zh: Getting Government AI Engineers to Tune into AI Ethics Seen as Challenge

articles.title: Novelty In The Game Of Go Provides Bright Insights For AI And Autonomous Vehicles
articles.description: By Lance Eliot, the AI Trends Insider   We already expect that humans to exhibit flashes of brilliance. It might not happen all the time, but the act itself is welcomed and not altogether disturbing when it occurs.    What about when Artificial Intelligence (AI) seems to display an act of novelty? Any such instance is bound to get our attention; […]]]>
articles.title_zh: Novelty In The Game Of Go Provides Bright Insights For AI And Autonomous Vehicles

articles.title: The Cognitive Categorical Transformer: Category-Theoretic Inductive Biases for Language Modeling
articles.description:
arXiv:2605.28864v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The Cognitive Categorical Transformer (CCT) is a 306M-parameter architecture that augments a pretrained GPT-2 Small backbone with cognitively grounded components derived from category theory and several inspirations from cognitive science. Under a matched-step protocol (215,000 optimizer steps, matched data, matched optimizer and schedule) on WikiText-103, CCT reaches 21.27 validation perplexity, compared with 24.19 for an identically fine-tuned G
articles.title_zh: The Cognitive Categorical Transformer: Category-Theoretic Inductive Biases for Language Modeling

articles.title: Promise and Perils of Using AI for Hiring: Guard Against Data Bias
articles.description: By AI Trends Staff   While AI in hiring is now widely used for writing job descriptions, screening candidates, and automating interviews, it poses a risk of wide discrimination if not implemented carefully.  That was the message from Keith Sonderling, Commissioner with the US Equal Opportunity Commision, speaking at the AI World Government event held live and virtually in […]]]>
articles.title_zh: Promise and Perils of Using AI for Hiring: Guard Against Data Bias

articles.title: Predictive Maintenance Proving Out as Successful AI Use Case
articles.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor   More companies are successfully exploiting predictive maintenance systems that combine AI and IoT sensors to collect data that anticipates breakdowns and recommends preventive action before break or machines fail, in a demonstration of an AI use case with proven value.   This growth is reflected in optimistic market forecasts. […]]]>
articles.title_zh: Predictive Maintenance Proving Out as Successful AI Use Case

articles.title: Digital Natives Seen Having Advantages as Part of Government AI Engineering Teams
articles.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor   AI is more accessible to young people in the workforce who grew up as ‘digital natives’ with Alexa and self-driving cars as part of the landscape, giving them expectations grounded in their experience of what is possible.   That idea set the foundation for a panel discussion at AI World […]]]>
articles.title_zh: Digital Natives Seen Having Advantages as Part of Government AI Engineering Teams

articles.title: Adopt $\neq$ Adapt: Longitudinal Analyses of LLM Conversations in the Wild
articles.description:
arXiv:2605.29018v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Although a growing body of research has begun to describe user--LLM interactions, the picture it paints is largely static; little is known about how individual users change their behavior over time. To address this gap, we analyze the conversational trajectories of $\sim$12,000 randomly sampled Microsoft Bing Copilot users and compare these with data from WildChat-4.8M. While the Copilot data contains significant population-level trends, we find t
articles.title_zh: Adopt $\neq$ Adapt: Longitudinal Analyses of LLM Conversations in the Wild

articles.title: Ultra-Reduced-Impact-Encased-Logging (URIEL): propose a new method for selective sustainable logging and post-harvest silvicultural treatment in tropical forest using airborne robotics systems
articles.description:
arXiv:2605.28883v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Tropical forests worldwide are under intense deforestation pressure driven by economic and political interests, and scientific evidence suggests this deforestation contributes to climate change. This paper proposes a novel logging method for tropical forests, Ultra-Reduced-Impact-Encased-Logging (URIEL). This new method is based on heli-logging techniques combined with intensive use of robotics and AI integrated with post-harvest silvicultural tre
articles.title_zh: Ultra-Reduced-Impact-Encased-Logging (URIEL): propose a new method for selective sustainable logging and post-harvest silvicultural treatment in tropical forest using airborne robotics systems

articles.title: Frontier LLM-based agents can overcome the ontology curation bottleneck for natural phenotypes
articles.description:
arXiv:2605.28965v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Linking free-text phenotype descriptions to ontology terms, typically referred to as phenotype annotation, is essential for the cross-study integration of comparative morphological data. This labor intensive process has heavily relied on highly trained human experts, which makes it challenging to scale and thus a key bottleneck. Dahdul et al. (2018) established a Gold Standard (GS) of Entity-Quality (EQ) annotations across seven phylogenetic studi
articles.title_zh: Frontier LLM-based agents can overcome the ontology curation bottleneck for natural phenotypes

articles.title: When Models Disagree: Rethinking LLM Evaluation for Public Comment Analysis
articles.description:
arXiv:2605.29025v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Federal agencies are deploying large language models (LLMs) to categorize public comment corpora, where the model's organization of the record shapes what policymakers see and which arguments register. Standard evaluation, anchored on stance accuracy against a small validated set, cannot detect when different models produce materially different categorizations of the same public input. We propose an Interpretive Audit Pipeline that treats multi-mo
articles.title_zh: When Models Disagree: Rethinking LLM Evaluation for Public Comment Analysis

articles.title: VFEAgent: A Multimodal Agent Framework for End-to-End Automated Finite Element Analysis
articles.description:
arXiv:2605.28978v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Finite Element Analysis (FEA) serves as the cornerstone of modern engineering design. However, its workflow is inherently complex and relies heavily on domain expertise. Although recent efforts have integrated Large Language Models (LLMs) into FEA, existing approaches face limitations in handling multimodal inputs and executing complex tasks. To address these limitations, we propose VFEAgent, an end-to-end multi-agent system designed to automate F
articles.title_zh: VFEAgent: A Multimodal Agent Framework for End-to-End Automated Finite Element Analysis
